title: scoparia flava var. pinnatifida
intro: Scoparia flava var. pinnatifida, also known as Chinese broom grass or Japanese tea plant, is a perennial herb native to East Asia and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and aiding in digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Scoparia flava var. pinnatifida is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese herbal medicine.
